title: ceropegia claviloba
intro: Ceropegia claviloba is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the forked milkweed or forked waxflower. It is known for its unique appearance, with white or pale yellow flowers that are shaped like a fork, and its milky latex. Ceropegia claviloba has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and topical applications to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
